Handtrack.js是一个用于直接在浏览器中对实时手部检测（边界框）进行原型制作的库。在下面，它使用了经过训练的卷积神经网络，该网络提供了图像中手的位置的边界框预测。卷积神经网络（ssdlite，mobilenetv2）使用tensorflow对象检测api进行训练（请参阅此处）。
  这项工作基于coco-ssd tensorflowjs示例。如果您有兴趣检测/跟踪coco数据集中的90个类别中的任何一个，请务必进行检查。
 提供该库作为有用的包装，以允许您在Web应用程序中原型基于手/手势的交互。无需了解。它接受一个html图像元素（例如img，视频，画布元素），并返回一个边界框，类名和置信度分数的数组。
 该库还提供了一些有用的功能（例如getFPS获取FPS，renderPredictions在画布元素上绘制边界框）以及可自定义的模型参数。
    使用自我手数据集进行训练。当您从俯视图（自我观）观看图像中的手时，您会注意到该模型工作得更好。
  模型封装在npm包中，可以使用jsdelivr进行访问，jsdelivr是免费的开源CDN，可让您在Web应用程序中包括任何npm包。您可能会注意到，在第一次打开页面时，模型加载缓慢，但是在随后的加载（缓存）中加载速度更快。
   如果您对基于手势的原型（作为输入的身体）交互体验感兴趣，那么Handtrack.js可能会有用。 Usser不需要附加任何其他传感器或硬件，但可以立即利用基于手势和输入为输入的身体交互所带来的互动优势。 
当手和其他对象的重叠可以代表有意义的交互信号时（例如，对象的触摸或选择事件）。
 场景中的人的手可以充当活动识别的代理（例如，从下棋者的视频或图像中自动跟踪运动活动）。或者简单地计算图像或视频帧中有多少个人。
 您想要一个易于访问的演示，任何人都可以通过最少的设置轻松运行或试用。

  该项目中使用的对象检测模型是使用人的手的带注释的图像进行训练的（请参见此处），并转换为tensorflow.js格式。该包装器库是使用准则和一些代码从coco-ssd tensorflowjs改编而成的。

如果您在项目/论文/研究中使用此代码，并且想引用此工作，请使用以下内容。  Victor Dibia，HandTrack：使用卷积神经网络对实时手部追踪接口进行原型设计的库，https：//github.com/victordibia/handtracking  @article {Dibia2017，作者= {Victor，Dibia}，标题= {HandTrack：使用卷积神经网络进行实时手部追踪接口原型的库}，年份= {2017}，发布者= {GitHub}，期刊= {GitHub存储库 }，网址= {https://github.com/victordibia/handtracking/tree/master/docs/handtrack.pdf}，}  这件事仍然很繁琐（您的粉丝可能会在一段时间后旋转）。 这主要是由于预测边界框所需的神经网络操作。 也许可能有方法可以改善/优化这一点。  跨框架跟踪ID。 也许一些巧妙的方法为每个ID分配ID，当它们进入一帧并对其进行跟踪时（例如，基于幼稚的欧几里得距离）。
note_en:
Handtrack.js is a library for prototyping realtime hand detection (bounding box), directly in the browser. Underneath, it uses a trained convolutional neural network that provides bounding box predictions for the location of hands in an image. The convolutional neural network (ssdlite, mobilenetv2) is trained using the tensorflow object detection api ( see here).
  This work is based on the  the coco-ssd tensorflowjs sample. Definitely check it out if you are interested in detecting/tracking any of the 90 classes in the coco dataset.
 The library is provided as a useful wrapper to allow you prototype hand/gesture based interactions in your web applications. without the need to understand. It takes in a html image element ( img,  video,  canvas elements, for example) and returns an array of bounding boxes, class names and confidence scores.
 The library also provides some useful functions (e.g  getFPS to get FPS,  renderPredictions to draw bounding boxes on a canvas element), and customizable model parameters.
    Trained using egohands dataset. You will notice the model works better when the hands in an image is viewed from a top (egocentic) view.
  Model is wrapped into an npm package, and can be accessed using  jsdelivr, a free open source cdn that lets you include any npm package in your web application. You may notice the model is loaded slowly the first time the page is opened but gets faster on subsequent loads (caching).
   If you are interested in prototyping gesture based (body as input) interactive experiences, Handtrack.js can be useful. The usser does not need to attach any additional sensors or hardware but can immediately take advantage of engagement benefits that result from gesture based and body-as-input interactions.
    When an overlap of hand and other objects can represent meaningful interaction signals (e.g a touch or selection event for an object).
 Scenarios where the human hand motion can be a proxy for activity recognition (e.g. automatically tracking movement activity from a video or images of individuals playing chess). Or simply counting how many humans are present in an image or video frame.
 You want an accessible demonstration that anyone can easily run or tryout with minimal setup.
    <!-- Load the handtrackjs model. --> < script  src=" https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/handtrackjs/dist/handtrack.min.js" >  </ script > <!-- Replace this with your image. Make sure CORS settings allow reading the image! --> < img  id=" img"  src=" hand.jpg"/>  < canvas  id=" canvas"  class=" border" > </ canvas > <!-- Place your code in the script tag below. You can also use an external .js file --> < script >  // Notice there is no 'import' statement. 'handTrack' and 'tf' is  // available on the index-page because of the script tag above.  const  img  =  document . getElementById ( 'img' ) ;  const  canvas  =  document . getElementById ( 'canvas' ) ;  const  context  =  canvas . getContext ( '2d' ) ;  // Load the model.  handTrack . load ( ) . then ( model  =>  {  // detect objects in the image.  model . detect ( img ) . then ( predictions  =>  {  console . log ( 'Predictions: ' ,  predictions ) ;  } ) ;  } ) ; </ script >
   import *  as  handTrack  from  'handtrackjs' ; const  img  =  document . getElementById ( 'img' ) ; // Load the model. handTrack . load ( ) . then ( model  =>  {  // detect objects in the image.  console . log ( "model loaded" )  model . detect ( img ) . then ( predictions  =>  {  console . log ( 'Predictions: ' ,  predictions ) ;  } ) ; } ) ;
   Once you include the js module, it is available as  handTrack. You can then load a model with optional parameters.
 const  modelParams  =  {  flipHorizontal:  true ,  // flip e.g for video   imageScaleFactor:  0.7 ,  // reduce input image size for gains in speed.  maxNumBoxes:  20 ,  // maximum number of boxes to detect  iouThreshold:  0.5 ,  // ioU threshold for non-max suppression  scoreThreshold:  0.79 ,  // confidence threshold for predictions. } handTrack . load ( modelParams ) . then ( model  =>  { } ) ;
   model.detect takes an input image element (can be an  img,  video,  canvas tag) and returns an array of bounding boxes with class name and confidence level.
   [ {  bbox:  [ x ,  y ,  width ,  height ] ,  class:  "hand" ,  score:  0.8380282521247864 } ,  {  bbox:  [ x ,  y ,  width ,  height ] ,  class:  "hand" ,  score:  0.74644153267145157 } ]
  model.renderPredictions(predictions, canvas, context, mediasource): draw bounding box (and the input mediasource image) on the specified canvas.  predictions are an array of results from the  detect() method.  canvas is a reference to a html canvas object where the predictions should be rendered,  context is the canvas 2D context object,  mediasource a reference to the input frame (img, video, canvas etc) used in the prediction (it is first rendered, and the bounding boxes drawn on top of it).
    startVideo(video) : start webcam video stream on given video element. Returns a promise that can be used to validate if user provided video permission.
    The start script launches a simple  python3 webserver from the demo folder using  http.server. You should be able to view it in your browser at  http://localhost:3005/. You can also view the pong game control demo on same link  http://localhost:3005/pong.html
  The object detection model used in this project was trained using annotated images of the human hand ( see here) and converted to the tensorflow.js format. This wrapper library was created using guidelines and some code adapted from  the coco-ssd tensorflowjs.
  Paper abstract of the  paper is here. (a full paper will be added when complete).
 If you use this code in your project/paper/research and would like to cite this work, use the below.
 Victor Dibia, HandTrack: A Library For Prototyping Real-time Hand TrackingInterfaces using Convolutional Neural Networks,  https://github.com/victordibia/handtracking
 @article{ Dibia2017,  author =   {Victor, Dibia },  title =   {HandTrack: A Library For Prototyping Real-time Hand TrackingInterfaces using Convolutional Neural Networks },  year =   {2017 },  publisher =   {GitHub },  journal =   {GitHub repository },  url =   {https://github.com/victordibia/handtracking/tree/master/docs/handtrack.pdf }, }
  This thing is still compute heavy (your fans may spin after while). This is mainly because of the neural net operations needed to predict bounding boxes. Perhaps there might be ways to improve/optimize this.
 Tracking id's across frames. Perhaps some nifty methods that assigns ids to each had as they enter a frame and tracks them (e.g based on naive euclidean distance).
title: Handtrack.js：一个用于在浏览器中进行实时手部检测原型的库
title_en: Handtrack.js: A library for prototyping realtime hand detection in the browser

超人是每月$ 30美元的邀请电子邮件客户。人们喜欢它的时尚设计，响应速度和键盘快捷键套件，以提高用户的电子邮件效率。对于每天花几个小时在电子邮件上的人来说，超人是值得的价格。它提供的功能的一部分包括读取状态，这对于希望保持对话状态的主管和销售人员很有用。
  用户喜欢超人类。在Twitter 1上迅速获得公司的热烈赞誉。 Andreessen Horowitz领投了该公司的最新一轮投资，估值约为2.6亿美元。
 紧随这个狂热的电子邮件客户的狂风暴雨之后，它迅速席卷了硅谷和Twitter，随后出现了一系列自称为“ x的超人”的公司。 2
 托德·戈德堡（Todd Goldberg）指出，“ X的超人”具有以下特征：
     我介绍了Sublime Text：比Superhuman早十多年的一款软件，它具有上述特性。 Sublime可以主要通过键盘快捷键来驱动。它的设计经过深思熟虑，就像我一直可耻地不愿意以80美元的价格购买非试用版一样。相似之处远不止于此。 Sublime有两个接近即时的搜索栏：一个用于在项目[goto]中打开文件，另一个用于执行命令。
  Sublime Text也可以同时使用，但也可以配置。对于程序员来说，通过GOTO（⌘+ P）和项目搜索（⌘+ Shift + F）的组合，就可以在一个大型项目上安装，打开和“运行”，这非常简单。
   毫不费力地，我提出了一个案例研究样本“ X的崇高文本”。 
我期待着光明黑暗的未来，通过键盘快捷键可以快速高效地完成所有任务。
note_en:
Superhuman is an invite-only $30/month email client. People like it for its sleek design, responsive speed, and suite of keyboard shortcuts to improve a user’s email efficiency. Superhuman is worth the price for people who spend hours on email every day. Part of its feature offering includes read statuses, which is useful for executives and sales people who like to keep a pulse on their conversations.
  Users love Superhuman. Take a quick peak on Twitter  1 at the effusive praise the company gets. Andreessen Horowitz led the latest investment round in the company with a valuation of roughly $260 million.
 Following in the tailwind of this prosumer email client that has taken Silicon Valley and Twitter by storm are a suite of companies claiming to be “Superhuman for x”.  2
 As identified by Todd Goldberg, the “Superhuman of X” has the following traits:
     I present Sublime Text: a piece of software more than a decade older than Superhuman, that shares the above attributes. Sublime can be driven primarily by keyboard shortcuts. It’s as thoughtfully designed as I have been shamefully unwilling to purchase the non-trial version for 80 USD. The similarities lie much deeper than that. Sublime has two close-to-instant-speed search-bars: one for opening a file in your project [goto] and one for executing commands.
  Sublime Text is also simultaneously opinionated but also configurable. It is simple enough for a programmer to install, open, and “hit the ground running” on a big project with a combination of GOTO (⌘+P) and project search (⌘+Shift+F).
   Without futhur ado, I present a case study sampling of “Sublime Texts for X”.
        I look forward to a  bright dark future where everything can be done fast and efficiently through keyboard shortcuts.
title: “ X的崇高文字”

只是一个超级快速的帖子，它提供了一个问题的一点点背景信息，一些（但不是全部）人正在看到您记录的GPS轨迹在哪里被向上偏移一百米或更多。通常，您的路线模式是正确的，但可能会偏向一侧或另一侧。但是，在很多情况下，通过完成锻炼，它会自行解决。换句话说，这主要是一次性的问题。
 这个问题似乎在很大程度上影响着在其设备中使用Sony GPS芯片组的公司，这些公司基本上是过去几年中所有的新Garmin设备，过去几年中的大多数新Suunto设备，过去几年中的所有Polar设备，所有COROS设备，Wahoo RIVAL等。除了苹果，其他人或多或少。
 受影响设备的清单非常粗略（我在这里一吨不见，这只是最受欢迎的设备）
 – COROS GPS手表（尽管需要仔细检查原始Pace）– Garmin Forerunner 45/245/745/945 / Fenix 6 / MARQ / Vivoactive 3/4 / Venu – Garmin Edge 130 Plus 530/830/1030 Plus – Polar Vantage V / M / V2，Grit X，Ignite – Suunto 5＆amp; Suunto 9 – Wahoo RIVAL
 再说一次，我在这里错过了很多船，但是那是我头顶上的大笔钱。
 自2018年左右以来，所有这些公司都已改用Sony GPS芯片组，Polar和Suunto最初加入了此行，随后紧随其后的是Garmin，COROS等。索尼GPS芯片组被这些公司广泛使用，因为它可以节省电能，从而延长电池寿命。
 但是，这不仅仅是要点，而不是问题的原因。此问题与星历数据文件有关，星历数据文件也称为EPO文件（扩展的预测轨道）或连接的预测星历（CPE）。或者只是卫星预缓存文件。该文件是经常（通常每隔几天）发送到您的设备的文件。该文件使您的手表在外出时几乎可以立即找到GPS卫星。基本上，这是一份关于接下来几天或长达一周左右的时间里卫星的位置的备忘单。 
您的手表或自行车计算机会根据您连接手表的方式自动通过手机，WiFi或USB通过Bluetooth Smart获取此文件。大多数公司会在您的手表同步并需要新版本时提供它。因此，从您的角度来看，您什么也不会做–它只是在后台悄悄发生。
 现在，此文件中的数据是错误的，因此手表在前几分钟使用的数据也是错误的-导致偏移。您可以从一个DCR阅读器中看到以下示例：
    由于手表在整个锻炼过程中都保持与GPS卫星的连接，因此从上方提取更新数据后，手表通常会进行自我校正。大多数人报告说，它会在锻炼结束或下一次锻炼之前解决。您可以从另一个DCR阅读器中看到：
 同样在这里–在我的Fenix 6X Pro和我妻子的Forerunner 45S的起点上出现相同的错误，然后他们自行解决–我走了，她跑了，所以我的路线更加准确，因为我有更多的时间来解决问题了……图片.twitter.com / tZ4ysgYPtW
  为解决此问题，您只需将手表/自行车计算机放在外面，让它记录一个小时的锻炼情况，然后将其丢弃即可。它会收到所需的卫星信息，以可能对其进行纠正。
 但这不会影响所有人。例如，我今天尝试使用两款基于Sony的手表（Garmin FR745和FR945）来确定它的走线方向，而事实并非如此。我怀疑这是因为在我的情况下，昨天和今天与我的孩子一起玩时，手表碰了几次，所以我想它已经重新下载了需要纠正的内容。我还尝试了两个非Sony芯片组（Fenix 5S和Apple Watch SE），也没有发现任何问题。
 在今天与Garmin交谈时，他们正在解决此问题，他们认为最有可能的是，他们只是从服务器更新了卫星预缓存数据，这又使您的手表能够像往常一样抓取。但是首先，他们（和其他人）正在努力找出导致此问题的数据到底出了什么问题。 
在与该行业的另一个人交谈时，他们指出，从技术上讲，2020年有53周，而这是第53周。 因此，可疑的Sony数据文件问题实际上可能与该复杂性有关。  看……正当您以为自己逃脱了2020年时，它仍在那儿等着您。
note_en:
Just a super quick post to provide a tiny bit of context on an issue some (but not all) folks are seeing where your recorded GPS track is offset by upwards of a hundred meters or more. In general, the pattern of your route is correct, but it may be displaced to one side or the other. However, in many cases by the completion of the workout, it sorts itself out. In other words, it’s mostly a one-time issue.
 The issue largely appears to be impacting companies that leverage the Sony GPS chipset in their devices, which is basically all new Garmin devices in the last few years, most new Suunto devices in the last few years, all Polar devices in the last few years, all COROS devices, the Wahoo RIVAL, and more. More or less everyone except Apple.
 Very rough list of impacted devices (I’m missing a ton here, these are just the most popular ones)
 – COROS GPS watches (need to double-check original Pace though) – Garmin Forerunner 45/245/745/945/Fenix 6/MARQ/Vivoactive 3/4/Venu – Garmin Edge 130 Plus 530/830/1030 Plus – Polar Vantage V/M/V2, Grit X, Ignite – Suunto 5 & Suunto 9 – Wahoo RIVAL
 Again, I’m missing a boatload here, but that’s the big-ticket ones off the top of my head.
 All these companies have switched to the Sony GPS chipset since around 2018, with Polar and Suunto initially jumping onboard, followed quickly thereafter by Garmin, COROS, and more. The Sony GPS chipset is widely used by these companies because of the power savings delivering longer battery life.
 But – that’s all besides the point, and not the cause of the issue. The issue has to do with the  ephemeris data file, also called the EPO file (Extended Prediction Orbit) or Connected Predictive Ephemeris (CPE). Or simply the satellite pre-cache file. That’s the file that’s delivered to your device on a frequent basis (usually every few days). This file is what makes your watch near-instantly find GPS satellites when you go outside. It’s basically a cheat-sheet of where the satellites are for the next few days, or up to a week or so.
 Your watch or bike computer automatically gets this file via Bluetooth Smart from your phone, WiFi, or USB, depending on how you connect your watch. Most companies deliver it anytime your watch syncs and needs a new version. So from your side, you never do anything – it just quietly happens in the background.
 Right now, the data in this file is wrong, and thus the data your watch uses for those first few minutes is also wrong – leading to the offsets. You can see an example of this below, from  one DCR reader:
    As the watch sustains its connection with GPS satellites over the length of the workout,  it’ll generally re-correct itself as it pulls in updated data from above. Most people are reporting that it’ll  resolve itself either by the end of the workout, or the next workout. You can see that here from yet another DCR reader:
 Same here – identical errors on start point on my Fenix 6X Pro and my wife’s Forerunner 45S, then they sort themselves out – I walked, she ran, so my route is more accurate as it had more time to get a fix I guess…  pic.twitter.com/tZ4ysgYPtW
  To help resolve this,  you can simply stick your watch/bike computer outside and let it record an hour workout, then just discard it after that. It’ll have received the required satellite information to likely correct itself.
 But it won’t impact everyone. For example, I tried today with two Sony-based watches (Garmin FR745 and FR945) to get it to lay down an incorrect track – and it wouldn’t. I suspect this is because in my case the watch had been bumped on a few times while playing with my kids yesterday and today, so my guess is it had already re-downloaded what it needed to be correct out of the gate. I also tried with two non-Sony chipsets (Fenix 5S and Apple Watch SE), and didn’t see any problems either.
 In talking to Garmin today,  they’re working on a fix for the issue, that they believe will most likely be that they just update the satellite pre-cache data from the server, which in turn your watch will grab just as it always does. But  first they (and others) are working to sort out what exactly is wrong with the data that’s causing this.
 In talking to another person in the industry dealing with the issue, they noted that technically 2020 had 53 weeks, and this is the 53rd week. As such, the suspect Sony data file issue might actually be tied to that complexity.
 See…just when you thought you escaped 2020, it’s still there waiting for you at the start of your run.
title: 2021年新的GPS精度问题影响了一些Garmin，Suunto和其他GPS设备
title_en: New 2021 GPS Accuracy Issue Impacting Some Garmin, Suunto, Other GPS Devices

note:
要到达油鸟洞Cueva de los Tayos，请沿着厄瓜多尔的首都基多向东行驶八个小时，途经狭窄，崎hol不平的道路，蜿蜒穿过亚马逊盆地上方的云雾林。停在门德斯（Mendez）小镇外面，沿着一条小路前往浑浊的圣地亚哥河（Santiago River）河岸，在那里您会看到当地人的肩膀上拖着150磅蒲式耳的香蕉。将自己放低到一个长长的木制独木舟中，滑过级联的瀑布，直到一条土路的起点。在湿度较高的地方远足五小时，越过蓬蒂亚·德·科安格斯山，然后到达Bocana de Coangos的山顶。这条小路的尽头是一个空旷的三间茅草屋，那里是十二个守护山洞的古老部落Shuar的所在地。
 Shuar是该地区的土著人民，他们是传说中的战士，以萨满教和缩小敌人的头颅而闻名。塔约斯人在由部落管理和保护的深处领土内招手，游客在浏览当地政治和习俗时必须格外小心。厄瓜多尔武装大学的地质学教授兼研究员西奥·托卡里迪斯（Theo Toulkaridis）是Tayos的主要专家，他在2014年以艰难的方式学到了这一点。在探索洞穴后，他爬上去发现了20只愤怒的舒阿尔在等他。 Toulkaridis雇用了当地向导，但是其他Shuar感到不高兴，因为他们也没有被雇用。 Toulkaridis回忆说：“我的向导紧紧地拥抱我，'不要抗拒'。”然后，一名Shuar女人用皮带鞭打他。
 塔约斯（Tayos）以棕色羽毛，钩状嘴的夜行鸟类而得名，它们与数千只蝙蝠一起栖息在洞穴内。鸟类的行为很像蝙蝠，它们在黑暗中度过了一天，并在夜间前往觅食。它们之所以被称为油鸟，是因为它们的脂肪小鸡被Shuar捕获并减少为油。也有传言说这个山洞里有失落文明的文物。瑞士作家埃里希·冯·丹尼肯（Erich vonDäniken）于1972年最畅销的一本书，名为《众神的黄金》，声称塔约斯（Tayos）拥有雕刻过的通道和用未知语言书写的平板电脑的“金属图书馆”。冯·丹尼克（VonDäniken）长期以来一直相信外星人曾经居住在地球上，而这些平板电脑也符合他的理论，即外星人帮助古代人进化。这个概念被批评为伪科学和种族主义，将现在边缘化的地球人的成就归功于太空闯入者。然而，它催生了许多书籍，会议和电视节目的家庭手工业，包括历史频道的《古代外星人》，该频道于2010年首播，是该网络最受欢迎的节目之一。
 在出版《众神的金子》的几年后，已故的苏格兰探险家斯坦·霍尔（Stan Hall）组建了一个由100位科学家，洞穴探险者，英国和厄瓜多尔军事人员以及著名的宇航员尼尔·阿姆斯特朗（Neil Armstrong）组成的团队，他们进入塔约斯（Tayos）揭开谜底。他们发现的东西使他们惊讶。在内部的深处，在那些无法挂耳的地方，有些石头通道似乎被切成直角然后进行了抛光。他们还发现了可以追溯到公元前1500年的墓地。
 “尸体好像被这么多孤独的世纪后的突如其来的入侵所吓倒，一摸摸就崩溃了，”霍尔在《远洋志》中写道。尽管团队没有找到金属库，但阿姆斯特朗将冒险“带上了月球到那里”。
 除此之外，只有少数勇敢的远足者，睁大眼睛的UFO信徒，甚至是百翰杨大学的一组研究人员（他们相信金属片可能与摩门教徒的信仰联系在一起），才融入其中。该洞穴还引起了地质学家和考古学家的兴趣，他们已经使用3D技术绘制了部分地图以更好地了解其范围。 （到目前为止，已经绘制了该洞穴大约四英里的地图，但估计仍然有三英里。）Toulkaridis称其为“一个根本没有被人接触过的天然实验室。”
 要进入Tayos，您需要的不仅仅是Shuar的许可。您还需要洞穴本身的祝福。我在2019年8月一个繁星点点的夜晚学习到了这个小型的Shuar居民点Kuankus，距离Tayos约一英里。到这里来一直是酷热十小时的残酷跋涉。跋涉包括穿越急流上方高高的摇摇欲坠的绳桥，并穿过装满巨型黑子弹蚂蚁的茂密丛林在泥泞中跋涉，之所以命名，是因为被一个人咬一口就像被枪杀一样。计划是在Kuankus过夜，然后在第二天早晨与Shuar向导一起进入洞穴。 
我在这里，由斯坦·霍尔（Stan Hall）34岁的苏格兰半厄瓜多尔裔女儿，艾琳·霍尔（Eileen Hall）领导的一个小团队，在父亲死于前列腺癌后，父亲继续寻求了解洞穴的真实历史和力量的努力艾琳（Eileen）于2008年居住在伦敦，他具有艺术和精神修养，是一位前建筑师，现在与私人客户进行她所谓的能量修复工作。她与另一位建筑师Tamsin Cunningham一起，还是Tayos的共同创始人，该公司通过写作，音乐和冥想探索洞穴。今天，她的长长的棕色头发扎成马尾辫，穿着黑色的厄瓜多尔衬衫，长长的灰色远足裤和蓝色的泥泞橡胶靴。这是她的第四次探险。当我问她应该期待什么时，她告诉我Tayos是“一种迷幻的经历”。
 从木碗奇卡（chicha）中，饮后，奇卡是一种由发酵的尤卡（由尤卡（yuca）咀嚼然后吐入碗中的妇女制成）制成的含酒精的白垩饮料，然后我们与Shuar一起围坐在篝火旁。巫师（一个长而黑头发的矮胖，中年妇女）带领我们进行了许可仪式。我们用西班牙语表示感谢，她感谢星星，月亮和地球。她从火上移走了闷烧的原木，在我们每个人的祝福中挥舞着烟雾。最后，我们轮流请求Shuar宗教中的强大力量Arutam进入Tayos。沉默了片刻之后，萨满祭司告诉我们，精神使我们进入了她所谓的“大地之子宫”。
 街头小贩咬着骨头说：“ Morder la cabeza”，当他递给我的时候，是一个蓬勃的白色g虫，它是从铁板烧的烤架上新鲜出来的。当我努力将头部与身体区分开时，艾琳在她的苏格兰方言中说道：“那是黑色的小东西。”
 下降到塔约斯（Tayos）前几天，我们已经停下来前往梅拉（Mera），这是通往安第斯山脉的门户小城。一支乐队在城镇广场附近吹鼓和吹小号，当地商人在那里出售手工艺品和食物。艾琳（Eileen）摇曳着节日音乐，并在她的蝴蝶耳环上赞美了当地的女工匠。尽管艾琳从小就一直在英国居住，但她出生于厄瓜多尔，与该国有着深厚的联系。如果不是几十年前把父亲吸引到这里的塔约斯（Tayos），她甚至不存在。
 艾琳的父亲从未梦想过成为著名的探险家。在爱丁堡附近的海边小镇邓巴（Dunbar）结婚时，他是位谦虚的土木工程师，对科学，历史和旅行抱有浓厚的兴趣。 “他对探险家产生了兴趣，”艾琳说。 “像阿拉伯的劳伦斯这样的人会进入未知的世界。”阅读《众神的金子》中的塔约斯，获得了他前所未有的想象力。冯·丹尼克（VonDäniken）声称，阿根廷和匈牙利的探险家胡安·莫里兹（Juan Moricz）将他带到了山洞里，他们在那里发现了这些碑文，“可能包含了人类历史的摘要，也描述了人类的起源。人类在地球上和消失的文明的信息。”
 霍尔的奇幻故事吸引了他，他一时心血来潮地决定写信给尼尔·阿姆斯特朗（Neil Armstrong），并邀请他在1976年去洞穴旅行。霍尔了解到，宇航员具有苏格兰血统，因此他可能会考虑这个想法。令霍尔震惊的是，阿姆斯特朗回信说他很感兴趣。霍尔带着这封信，与英国和厄瓜多尔政府进行了接触，后者同意提供资金和直升机运送到现场。不到一年，霍尔组织了他当时最大的洞穴探险之一。
  霍尔的旅程使他出名，激发了他辞去日常工作，搬到厄瓜多尔继续探索这个洞穴。但这也结束了他的第一次婚姻，无法忍受这种压力。在基多，他会见了厄瓜多尔妇女JanethMuñoz，并与之结婚，她对厄瓜多尔的历史充满了激情，并于1986年生下了他们的女儿。对于艾琳来说，成长为著名探险家的孩子就像童话故事一样。但是仍然住在爱丁堡附近的穆尼奥斯说，霍尔努力争取他的研究的支持。他被传说中的图书馆所困扰，而他们唯一的钱来自他偶尔的咨询工作。当他不与啤酒商讨寻宝策略时，他与Shuar在一起，后者使他成为该部落的血腥兄弟。 “爸爸变得痴迷，他称它为发烧，”艾琳回忆道。在厄瓜多尔正处于严重的金融危机边缘的时候，霍尔如此忙于工作，他们常常无法支付房租。 
Hall和Muñoz育有第二个女儿，在1990年代中期，Muñoz坚持将家庭搬到苏格兰，以拥有一个更安全，更稳定的生活。对于10岁的艾琳（Eileen）而言，搬迁-苏格兰冬季中期-具有毁灭性。她是班上唯一的棕褐色，说西班牙语的孩子。在其他学生的欺凌下，她变得沮丧，甚至自杀。
 当她长大成人后，艾琳便采取了措施以寻找生活中的意义。她环游欧洲并获得建筑学位。同时，她的父亲继续返回厄瓜多尔，在那里他对金属图书馆的无能为力的搜索越来越令人沮丧。艾琳得知，他也死于癌症。
 2008年的一个深夜，当她在苏格兰的床边守夜时，她被一种新的目标感震惊：她将继续他的工作。对她来说，塔约斯（Tayos）不仅是Shuar的奥秘，而且还与她的血统联系在一起。她答应了他，将返回厄瓜多尔，并继续他开始的旅程。 “我会跟随你的精神，”她去世前她说。
 艾琳的决定让母亲充满了喜怒无常。穆尼奥斯说：“我为她感到骄傲，但我想她会遇到与他一样的麻烦，”她试图保护这个洞穴。
  2010年之后，Shuar开始引导小群游客进入Tayos。然后，在2015年，艾琳与厄瓜多尔纪录片电影制片人首次踏入洞穴。她很兴奋，但旅途的现实却充满危险。那是雨季，这意味着在进近时，洪水和水顺着泥泞的斜坡滑落。进入Tayos时，首先要用一系列绳子的吊索开始，而下降后的Eileen则被潜伏在黑色环境中的尖叫油鸟吓倒了。到达地面后，她发现该洞是如此尘土飞扬，漆黑一片，以至于她在夜幕降临之前就跳了出来。她说：“太多了。”
 两年后的2017年，她受邀返回旅游频道节目Expedition Unknown的一集。尽管她从小就被Tayos称为“宝藏故事”，但是她不再相信金属图书馆的存在，即使存在，她也不认为这是小矮人的作品。现在，她觉得自己像是在超负荷的真人秀中的道具。她无法忍受主持人对Shuar的残酷对待，以及他对相机的抢劫抢劫：当他进入一个阴暗的房间时，他宣称：“这真是真的！”
 在以男性为主的冒险故事中，艾琳感到越来越疏远。从哥伦布到沙克尔顿再到尼尔·阿姆斯特朗的探险家故事主要是白人在寻求已知世界的边缘。她适合在哪里？她告诉我：“男人下马发现了新土地，这就是为什么我们的历史是妇女必须待在家里。” “对我来说，这次旅程一直是在寻找我作为女人的声音。” 
在2018年8月，她带领她的第一次探险前往Tayos，这次旅行既具有艺术​​目标又具有科学目标。她不想只是调查洞穴；她想找到新的体验方式。为此，她请来了一群艺术家，其中包括来自英国的电子音乐家乔恩·霍普金斯（Jon Hopkins）和神经科学家孟德尔·凯伦（Mendel Kaelen），后者将衡量在洞穴中听音乐如何影响大脑。
 进入室内后，艾琳和艺术家进入阴影中，冥想，播放音乐并聆听洞穴壁雕刻声音的方式。她说：“这是一种更温和的探索方式，而不是这种恭维方式。”
 四天后，她对这个地方和自己有了新的认识。她说：“在萨满式的工作中，您会进入黑暗和阴影，但逃避的那一刻是遇到麻烦的时候。” “对我来说，洞穴就是面对未知事物并与之成为朋友以获得更完整的生活智慧的想法。”
  在我们的旅行中，Eileen有一个愿景，一个将创造力和行动主义结合在一起的项目。该计划是为了在伦敦的多媒体装置中录制洞穴内的音乐表演。宣传将为联合国教科文组织世界遗产地的指定提供支持，保护塔约斯周边地区免受采矿和其他威胁。音乐家是厄瓜多尔的多乐器演奏家，名叫戴维·比利亚戈麦斯（David Villagomez），英国制作人亨利·“霍菲”·霍夫曼。我们将在营地中徒步一小时，然后在洞穴中度过两天两夜，在Shuar的指导下进行记录和探索。
 Eileen承认怀疑论者可能会给她贴上New Agey的标签，但Toulkaridis告诉我，在最初抵制她在山洞里演奏音乐的想法后，他对企业的深奥性质没有异议。对他来说，任何有助于传播有关该地区景观的脆弱性和Shuar人民的话都是值得的。他说：“科学家们没有接触大众媒体。” “您需要一个可以帮助您传达信息的人。”
 舒阿尔人族是一个游牧民族的多族裔部落，遍布厄瓜多尔和秘鲁的森林，自16世纪西班牙人入侵其领土以来，一直在与殖民化和传教活动进行斗争。对欧洲人来说，他们是迷恋的对象：一种战士文化，以缩小受害者的头部作为俘获灵魂的方式而闻名。在战斗中，他们认为萨满巫师可以制造出隐形飞镖来摧毁敌人。他们如此恐惧，以至于1995年厄瓜多尔军队发展了Shuar士兵的精锐部队与秘鲁进行领土战。
 自1960年代以来，shuar一直对其部分土地保持管辖权，它们已成为保护该国自然资源的强大力量。 2008年，他们帮助说服厄瓜多尔立法机关通过了世界上第一部法律，授予了通常为人类保留的森林和河流各种法律权利。 
尽管如此，Shuar仍很容易发展。 2012年，政府与Ecuacorriente S.A.签订了一项合同，由这家中国国有矿业公司投资14亿美元，在Shuar领土的心脏地带建设一座铜金矿。据国际人权联合会和联合国称，此举违反了厄瓜多尔法律和关于土著人民权利的国际协定。该矿被称为Mirador矿，是该国第一个大规模采矿作业，将占用约22,000英亩的森林。自开始施工以来，Shuar举行了多次抗议活动。与警察和军队发生了暴力对抗，至少有两名反雷活动分子被谋杀。
 Eileen告诉我，她的父亲赢得了Shuar的尊敬，该部落帮助他获得了进入洞穴的帮助。她说：“他们非常热情。”在过去三天去过Tayos之后，她与Kuankus村民建立了自己的家庭联系，他们身着现代服装，但仍然大部分生活在这片土地上。我们刚到时，一名叫Susana Wamputsar的Shuar中年妇女和她的丈夫Don Bosco Tiwiram向Eileen赠送了手工珠宝。
 仪式允许我们进入山洞之后，我们与这对夫妇以及他们的儿子之一Jaime（将成为我们的主要向导和翻译）一起坐在茅草屋外的小木板凳上。在Susana讲话时，Jaime将她的单词翻译成西班牙语，然后Eileen将其翻译成英语，所以我可以继续。当霍尔的探险队到达该地区时，苏珊娜（Susana）大约12岁，他回想起直升机降落的壮观景象。她说，当外国人在那里时，她社区的长者不敢去洞穴附近。她说，根据民间传说，这些人留下的是“大腿骨”以及一盒陶瓷。她讲述了另一个著名的故事，大约在几十年前，一位名叫卡洛斯·克雷斯皮神父的意大利僧侣带着“包裹在报纸上的药片”离开洞穴。
  Susana解释说，厄瓜多尔在霍尔探险之后不久就禁止远征Tayos，以便Shuar可以“回到他们的位置并与洞穴在一起”。它一直对外国人关闭，直到十年前Shuar开始提供旅行。当我问她Shuar相信Tayos里面的东西时，她告诉我，有一块高石头，上面放着盐，还有一种“双重精神”。其中一个是Nunkui，一个保护和种植该地区植物的女性实体。另一位是Weh，他制造了可治愈的盐。几个世纪以来，舒亚尔号（Shuar）会落入塔约斯（Tayos）来回收Weh的魔盐。但是杰米说，自从其他人开始在山洞中游荡之后，韦恩便退回到了阴影中。现在，他相信Weh准备返回。今年早些时候，Jaime听到一群高高的洞穴裂缝传来的巨响时，和一群游客一起在山洞里。他说：“这听起来像是脚步声。”
 结实而又瘦弱的海梅（Jaime）有着浓密的黑发和刘海，扭动他的前大灯，并与另一位向导一起，向下落的岩石刺向声音。在狭窄的通道上，脚步声越来越大，吓到另一个向导，以至于他退缩了，但海梅爬了进去。当一切突然变得寂静时，他独自一人。然后他低下头，看到了巨大的足迹。
  “我有一张照片，”他实际上是在回答。他在手机上滑动，然后将其交给我。这张照片确实显示出一块肥沃的棕色泥土足迹，据我估计，它有七个脚趾。
 如果海梅（Jaime）不太固执，而且话语不那么发自内心，那么他的口供就好像是个骗局。舒尔认为梦想是异象，而海梅说，好几个星期来，他一直在梦见韦在睡觉。那个以Shuar男人的形式出现的灵魂告诉Jaime他想回来。 
海梅（Jaime）说，他也一直梦想着艾琳（Eileen）回到塔约斯（Tayos），并带回别人。 他们将与海姆一起静静地坐在山洞深处，如果他们等待了足够长的时间，就会出现韦。 谈到艾琳，海梅告诉她，从身体上看，她可能住在伦敦，但她的真正精神是在塔约斯。 他说：“我觉得你住在山洞里。”  哦，还有一群梦his以求的人会和她在一起吗？ 那就是我们。  Tayos的河口在热带雨林的陡峭小路尽头张开。 在我们下山的早晨朝它走去时，蝴蝶在树叶间飞舞。 一条四英寸的毛毛虫，有黑色和黄色的条纹，还有尖尖的绿色头发，在一块破烂的原木中穿行。 那里  ......
note_en:
To get to Cueva de los Tayos—Cave of the Oilbirds—drive east out of Ecuador’s capital city of Quito for eight hours along narrow, potholed roads that twist through cloudforest above the Amazon Basin. Pull over outside the small town of Mendez, and walk a path to the bank of the muddy Santiago River, where you’ll see locals hauling 150-pound bushels of bananas on their shoulders. Lower yourself into a long wooden canoe and glide past cascading waterfalls to the start of a dirt trail. Hike five hours in the humidity, over Puntilla de Coangos mountain, then up to the summit of Bocana de Coangos. The trail ends at a clearing with three thatch huts, home to a dozen Shuar, the ancient tribe that guards the cave.
 The Shuar are the Indigenous people of the region, legendary warriors known for shamanism and for shrinking the heads of their enemies. Tayos beckons from deep inside territory that is managed and protected by the tribe, and visitors must take great care when navigating the local politics and customs. Theo Toulkaridis, a geology professor and researcher at the University of the Armed Forces in Ecuador, who is a leading expert on Tayos, learned this the hard way in 2014. After a few days exploring the cave, he climbed out to find 20 angry Shuar waiting for him. Toulkaridis had hired local guides, but other Shuar were upset that they had not been hired as well. “My guide hugged me close and whispered, ‘Don’t resist,’” Toulkaridis recalls. Then a Shuar woman whipped him with a belt.
 Tayos is named for the brown-feathered, hook-billed nocturnal birds that dwell inside the cave alongside thousands of bats. The birds act a lot like bats, spending their days in darkness and heading out at night to forage for fruit. They’re called oilbirds ­because of their fatty chicks, which the Shuar capture and reduce to oil. The cave is also rumored to contain artifacts of a lost civilization. A 1972 bestselling book by Swiss author Erich von Däniken, called   The Gold of the Gods, claimed that Tayos held carved passageways and a “metal library” of tablets written in an unknown language. Von Däniken has long believed that aliens once inhabited the earth, and the tablets fit his theory that extraterrestrials helped ancient people evolve. The notion has been criticized as pseudoscientific and racist, attributing the achievements of now marginalized earthlings to ­interlopers from space. Yet it spawned a cottage industry of books, conventions, and TV shows, ­including the History Channel’s  Ancient Aliens, which premiered in 2010 and is one of the network’s most popular programs.
 A couple of years after  The Gold of the Gods was published, the late Scottish explorer Stan Hall assembled a team of 100 scientists, cavers, British and Ecuadorean military personnel, and, remarkably, astronaut Neil Armstrong, who served as a figurehead, and led them into Tayos to unravel the mystery. What they found astonished them. Deep inside, in spots where it would have been impossible to lug machinery, there were stone passageways that appeared to have been cut at right angles and then polished. They also discovered a burial site dating back to 1500 B.C.
 “The cadaver, as if surprised by the sudden intrusion after so many lonely centuries, crumbled to dust when touched,” Hall writes in   Tayos Gold, his book about the expedition. Though the team didn’t find the metal library, Armstrong put the adventure “up there with the moon landing.”
 Beyond that, only a small number of intrepid hikers, wide-eyed UFO believers, and  even a team of researchers from Brigham Young University—who believed that the metal tablets might be linked to the Mormon faith—have made it inside. The cave has also attracted interest from geologists and archaeologists, who have mapped portions using 3D technology to better understand its scope. (Roughly four miles of the cave have been mapped so far, but an estimated three miles remain.) Toulkaridis calls it “a natural laboratory which is fundamentally untouched.”
 To enter Tayos, you need more than the permission of the Shuar. You also need a blessing from the cave itself. I learn this late one starry night in August of 2019, in Kuankus, the tiny Shuar settlement, which is located about one mile uphill from Tayos. Getting here has been a brutal ten-hour slog in sweltering heat. The trek included crossing a rickety rope bridge high above the rapids and trudging in mud through thick jungle loaded with giant black bullet ants, so named because being bit by one feels like being shot. The plan is to stay in Kuankus for the night, then enter the cave with our Shuar guides the next morning.
 I’m here with a small team led by Eileen Hall, Stan Hall’s 34-year-old half-Scottish, half-Ecuadoran daughter, who continued her father’s quest to understand the true history and power of the cave after he died of prostate cancer in 2008. Eileen, who lives in London, is artistic and spiritual, a former architect who now conducts what she calls energy-healing work with private clients. Along with another architect, Tamsin Cunningham, she is also a cofounder of  Tayos, a company that explores the cave through writing, music, and meditation. Today, with her long brown hair in a ponytail, she’s dressed in a black Ecuadorean shirt, long gray hiking pants, and blue rubber boots caked in mud. This is her fourth expedition to the cave. When I ask her what I should expect, she tells me that Tayos is “a psychedelic experience.”
 After sipping from a wooden bowl of chicha, a chalky, alcoholic drink made from fermented yuca (prepared by women who chew it and then spit into the bowl), we gather with the Shuar around a campfire. A shaman—a stout, middle-aged woman with long, dark hair—leads us through the permission ceremony. We hold hands while in Spanish she thanks the stars, the moon, the earth. She removes a smoldering log from the fire, waving its smoke behind each of us in a blessing. Finally, we take turns asking for permission from Arutam, the all-powerful force in the Shuar religion, to enter Tayos. After a few moments of silence, the shaman tells us that the spirit has allowed us inside what she calls “the womb of the earth.”
 “Morder la cabeza”—bite off the head—the street vendor says as he hands me a  chontacuro, a plump white grubworm fresh from a sizzling grill. As I struggle to distinguish the head from the body, Eileen says, in her Scottish brogue, “It’s that little black thing right there.”
 It’s a few days before our descent into Tayos, and we’ve stopped for a break in Mera, a small gateway town to the Andes. A band plays drums and trumpets just off the town square where local merchants sell crafts and food. Eileen sways to the festive music and compliments a local craftswoman on her butterfly earrings. Though Eileen has been living in the UK since she was a young girl, she was born in Ecuador and feels deeply connected to the country. If it wasn’t for Tayos, which drew her father here decades before, she wouldn’t even exist.
 Eileen’s father never dreamed of becoming a famous explorer. As a young married man in Dunbar, a seaside town near Edinburgh, he was a mild-mannered civil engineer with a bookish interest in science, history, and travel. “He got interested in explorers,” ­Eileen says. “People like Lawrence of Arabia who would go off into the unknown.” Reading about Tayos in  The Gold of the Gods captured his imagination like nothing before. Von Däniken claimed that an Argentine-Hungarian explorer, Juan Moricz, had taken him to the cave, where they found the tablets that, he wrote, “might contain a synopsis of the history of humanity, as well as an account of the origin of mankind on earth and information of a vanished civilization.”
 The fantastical account gripped Hall, who on a whim decided to write to Neil Armstrong and invite him to take a trip to the cave in 1976. Armstrong, recently world-famous from his moon walk, could draw enormous attention to the venture, and as Hall had learned, the astronaut had Scottish roots, so he just might consider the idea. To Hall’s shock, Armstrong wrote back saying he was interested. With that letter in hand, Hall approached both the British and Ecuadorean governments, which agreed to provide funding and helicopter transportation to the site. Within a year, Hall had organized one of the largest cave expeditions of his time.
  Hall’s journey made him famous, inspiring him to quit his day job and move to ­Ecuador to keep exploring the cave. But it also ended his first marriage, which couldn’t endure the strain. In Quito, he met and married an Ecuadoran woman, Janeth Muñoz, who shared his passion for Ecuador’s history, and in 1986 she gave birth to their daughter. For Eileen, growing up as the child of a celebrated explorer felt like a fairy tale. But Muñoz, who still lives near Edinburgh, says that Hall struggled to get support for his research. He was consumed by his search for the legendary library, and the only money they had came from his occasional consulting jobs. When he wasn’t strategizing with treasure hunters over beers, he was off with the Shuar, who made him a blood brother of the tribe. “Dad became obsessed, he called it the fever,” Eileen recalls. With Hall so caught up in his work while Ecuador was on the verge of a deep financial crisis, they were often unable to pay their rent.
 Hall and Muñoz had a second daughter, and in the mid-1990s Muñoz insisted on moving the family to Scotland to have a safer and more stable existence. For ten-year-old Eileen, the relocation—in the middle of the Scottish winter—was devastating. She was the only brown-skinned, Spanish-speaking child in her class. Bullied by other students, she became depressed and at times suicidal.
 As she matured into adulthood, Eileen took steps to find meaning in her life. She traveled around Europe and earned a degree in architecture. Meanwhile, her father continued to return to Ecuador, where his fruitless searches for the metal library grew increasingly frustrating. He was also, Eileen learned, dying of cancer.
 Late one night in 2008, while holding vigil at his bedside in Scotland, she was struck with a new sense of purpose: she would keep his work alive. For her, Tayos was about more than the mysteries of the Shuar—it was also a connection to her origins. She would go back to Ecuador, she promised him, and continue the journey he had begun. “I’m going to follow your spirit,” she said just before he died.
 Eileen’s decision left her mother with mixed emotions. “I feel proud of her,” Muñoz says, “but I suppose she is going to encounter the same kind of problems that he did,” with trying to protect the cave.
  After 2010, the Shuar began guiding small groups of tourists into Tayos. Then, in 2015, Eileen made her first trip into the cave with an Ecuadoran documentary filmmaker. She was excited, but the realities of the journey felt treacherous. It was the rainy season, which meant flooding streams and water coursing down muddy slopes along the approach hike. Entering Tayos begins with a rappel along a series of ropes, and on the descent Eileen was frightened by the screaming oilbirds lurking in the blackness. Once on the ground, she found the cave so overwhelmingly dusty and dark that she jumped at the first chance to leave before nightfall. “It was all too much,” she says.
 Two years later, in 2017, she was invited to return for an episode of  Expedition Unknown, a Travel Channel program. Though she had grown up with what she calls “the treasure story” of Tayos, she was no longer convinced that a metal library existed, and even if it did, she didn’t think it would be the work of little green men. Now she felt like a prop in an overwrought reality show. She couldn’t stomach the host’s perfunctory treatment of the Shuar and his blustery mugging for the camera: as he entered one shadowy chamber, he proclaimed, “This just got real!”
 Eileen felt a growing sense of alienation in a male-dominated adventure narrative. Explorer stories—from Columbus to Shackleton to Neil Armstrong—are predominantly about white men seeking the edges of the known world. Where did she fit in? “Men went off and discovered new lands, and that’s why our history is that women have to stay at home,” she told me. “For me this journey has been about finding my voice as a woman.”
 In August 2018, she led her first expedition to Tayos, a trip with both artistic and scientific goals. She didn’t want to just investigate the cave; she wanted to find new ways of experiencing it. To do this, she brought along a small group of artists, including Jon Hopkins, an electronic musician from England, and neuroscientist ­Mendel Kaelen, who would measure how listening to music in the cave impacted the brain.
 Once inside, Eileen and the artists made their way into the shadows, meditating, playing music, and listening to ways that the cave walls sculpted the sound. “It was a gentler way of exploring, instead of this gung-ho way,” she says.
 She emerged four days later with a new sense of the place, and of herself. “In shamanic work, you go into the darkness and the shadow, but the moment you run away from it is when you get into trouble,” she says. “For me the cave is this idea of facing the unknown and befriending it to receive a more complete wisdom about life.”
  For our trip, Eileen had a vision, a project that would bring together creativity and activism. The plan is to record musical performances inside the cave for a multimedia installa­tion in London. Publicity from that will generate support for Unesco World Heritage site designation, protecting the area around Tayos from mining and other threats. The musicians are an Ecuadoran multi-­instrumentalist named David Villa­gomez and British producer Henry “Hoffy” Hoffman. We’ll set up camp an hour’s hike into the cave and spend two days and nights there, recording and exploring under the guidance of the Shuar.
 Eileen admits that skeptics would probably label her New Agey, but Toulkaridis told me that, after initially being resistant to her idea of playing music in the cave, he has no objections to the esoteric nature of the enterprise. To him, anything that helps spread the word about the fragility of the region’s landscape and the Shuar people is worthwhile. “Scientists have no outreach to the mass media,” he says. “You need someone who can help get the message heard.”
 A seminomadic, polygynous tribe spread across the forests of Ecuador and Peru,  the Shuar have been fighting against colonization and missionization since the Spaniards invaded their territory in the 16th century. To the Europeans, they were subjects of fascination: a warrior culture known for shrinking the heads of their victims as a way of capturing souls. In battle they believed that their shaman could conjure invisible darts to destroy their foes. They were so feared that in 1995 the Ecuadorean army developed elite units of Shuar soldiers for territorial battles with Peru.
 Since the 1960s, the Shuar have maintained jurisdiction over portions of their land, and they’ve become a powerful force in protecting the country’s natural resources. In 2008,  they helped persuade the Ecuadorean legislature to pass the world’s first law granting the kinds of  legal rights to forests and rivers that are ordinarily reserved for people.
 Still, the Shuar are vulnerable to development. In 2012, the government  entered into a contract with Ecuacorriente S.A. in which the Chinese-owned mining company would invest $1.4 billion to build a copper and gold mine in the heart of Shuar territory. The move  defied Ecuadorean law and international agreements on the rights of Indigenous people, according to the ­ International Federation of Human Rights and the  United Nations. Called the Mirador Mine, it’s the first large-scale mining operation in the country and will  occupy some 22,000 acres of forest. Since construction began, the Shuar have staged  numerous protests. There have been violent confrontations with police and military forces, and at least two anti-mining activists have been  murdered.
 Eileen told me that her father earned the respect of the Shuar and that the tribe was instrumental in helping him gain access to the cave. “They were very welcoming,” she says. After three past trips to Tayos, she’s developed her own familial connection with Kuankus villagers, who dress in modern clothing but still mostly live off the land. When we first arrived, a middle-aged Shuar woman named Susana Wamputsar and her husband, Don Bosco Tiwiram, presented ­Eileen with handmade jewelry.
 After the ceremony giving us permission to enter the cave, we sit with the couple—along with one of their sons, Jaime, who will be our primary guide and translator—on a small wooden bench outside a thatch hut. As Susana speaks, Jaime translates her words into Spanish, then Eileen translates them into English so I can follow along. Susana, who was around 12 when Hall’s expedition came through the area, recalls the dramatic sight of the helicopters landing. She says the elders in her community wouldn’t dare go near the cave while the foreigners were there. According to folklore, the men left with “a thigh bone,” she says, as well as boxes of ceramics. She recounts another well-known story, about a time decades earlier when an Italian monk named Father Carlos Crespi left the cave with “tablets wrapped in newspaper.”
  Ecuador banned expeditions to Tayos soon after Hall’s expedition, so that the Shuar could “come back to their place and just be with the cave,” Susana explains. It remained closed to foreigners until the Shuar began offering tours a decade ago. When I ask her what the Shuar believe is inside Tayos, she tells me that there is a tall stone that contains salt, as well as a “double spirit.” One is Nunkui, a female entity who protects and cultivates the plants of the area. The other is Weh, who makes healing salts. For centuries, the Shuar would descend into Tayos to retrieve Weh’s magic salts. But since others began traipsing through the cave, says Jaime, Weh has receded into the shadows. Now he believes Weh is ready to return. Earlier in the year, Jaime was in the cave with a group of tourists when he heard a loud noise coming from a high cranny of a cavern. “It sounded like footsteps,” he says.
 Jaime, who is intense and wiry, with thick black hair and bangs, flipped on his ­headlamp and, along with another guide, shimmied over fallen rocks toward the sound. At a narrow passageway, the footsteps grew louder and louder, spooking the other guide so much that he retreated, but Jaime crawled inside. He was alone when everything suddenly went silent. Then he looked down and saw a giant footprint.
  “I have a picture,” he replies, matter-of-factly. He swipes at his phone, then hands it to me. The photo indeed shows a fat, wide footprint in brown mud that, by my count, has seven toes.
 His account would feel like a hoax if Jaime wasn’t so stoic and his words weren’t so heartfelt. The Shuar consider dreams to be visions, and for weeks, Jaime says, he has been seeing Weh in his sleep. The spirit, appearing in the form of a Shuar man, told Jaime that he wants to return.
 Jaime says he also had been having dreams of Eileen coming back to Tayos, bringing others with her. Together with Jaime, they would sit deep inside the cave in total silence, and if they waited long enough, Weh would emerge. Turning to Eileen, Jaime tells her that, physically, she may live in London, but her true spirit is in Tayos. “I feel that you live in the cave,” he says.
 Oh, and the group from his dreams who would conjure Weh with her? That would be us.

note:
科技巨头的免税大富翁在大西洋两岸都将终结，但让我们加快这一进程
   对技术行业做出预测总是很冒险的，但是今年看起来有所不同，至少就两个安全的赌注而言。其一是，去年开始对高科技巨头进行监管的尝试将会加强；其次，我们将越来越多地被来自Facebook＆amp; amp;他们试图避免民主遏制其不负责任的权力。
 在法规方面，去年在美国，谷歌的公司所有者Alphabet面临38个州和司法部的重大反托拉斯诉讼。在池塘的这一侧，正在为具有法定权力的数字市场部门做准备，这些部门将能够巧妙地避开关于什么构成数字时代的垄断这一棘手的定义性问题。取而代之的是，该部门将根据具体情况决定特定的科技公司是否具有“战略市场地位”，是否在至少一项数字活动中拥有“牢固的，根深蒂固的市场力量”，或者是否充当在线“门户” ”用于其他业务。而且，如果公司被判定为具有此身份，则将对其施加处罚和法规。
  在布鲁塞尔，欧盟提出了一个新的两方面的法律框架来遏制数字权力-《数字市场法》和《数字服务法》。 《数字市场法案》旨在遏制技术行业的反竞争做法（例如在潜在竞争对手扩大规模之前先对其进行收购），并将对侵权者处以全球收入10％的罚款。就《数字服务法》而言，它将责成社交媒体平台对其平台上的非法内容（诈骗，恐怖分子内容，滥用图片等）承担更多责任，为此，他们可能面临最高全球收入的6％的罚款。如果他们未能充分监管内容。因此，美国和英国的方法侧重于公司行为。欧盟的方法侧重于定义法律允许的内容。
 所有这些行动已经进行了很长一段时间，尽管很难确切说明如何发挥作用，但最重要的是，技术行业最终将成为受监管的行业。它的无法律禁令即将结束。
 乔·拜登（Joe Biden）担任高级管理人员的选择包括令人沮丧的前高科技公司支持者
 不过，最大的问题是：何时？由于问题的复杂性以及所涉公司的无底法律预算，反托拉斯行动的步伐令人鼓舞。法官在美国针对Google的一桩重大反托拉斯案中表示，他预计该案仅在2023年下半年才能出庭，然后可能会持续数年（就像Microsoft在1990年代的案件一样）。 
正如资深反垄断运动家马特·斯托勒（Matt Stoller）所指出的那样，与此有关的问题是，垄断行为持续的时间越长，损害就越大（例如，对收入被盗的广告商和财产被盗用的其他企业的损害）。正在完成。 Google去年的收入为1700亿美元，并且平均每年以10-20％的速度增长。按照保守的估计增长10％，该公司到2025年将再增加1000亿美元的收入，届时该案仍将上法庭。斯托勒说，“ Facebook今年的收入为800亿美元，但增长速度更快，因此收入的净增幅大致相当。换句话说，如果政府的主张是可信的，那么冗长的案子虽然可能是必要的，但也使这些垄断者每人再窃取1000亿美元。”
 如何加快将这些垄断者纳入考虑范围？一个关键因素是美国司法部提起诉讼的积极性。在2020年大选前夕，国会民主党人表现出对解决技术垄断的热情，但乔·拜登（Joe Biden）担任高级管理人员的选择包括令人沮丧的前科技公司支持者。和他的副总统当选人，卡马拉·哈里斯，始终视而不见硅谷巨头的反竞争收购在她的时间，加州总检察长。因此，如果人们希望美国新政府提出反托拉斯的热情，他们可能会感到失望。
 有趣的是，斯托勒（Stoller）提出，从1930年代开始，另一种方法（受美国信托破坏者的行动方式启发）可以对公司行为产生有益的影响。他指出，垄断不仅是非法的，“事实上，这是一种犯罪，是占统治地位的行为者侵占他人权利和财产的行为。漫长的审判从本质上讲类似于说银行抢劫犯要继续抢劫银行，直到他们被定罪并可能保留额外的赃物为止。”
 由于法治的基本原则是不应该犯罪，因此在反托拉斯行动中增加刑事指控的可能性可能会使人们的注意力集中在Facebook上，例如早上被绞死的可能性（约翰逊博士的步伐）。 ，谷歌，亚马逊和苹果。作为永恒的乐观主义者，我想不到2021年的前景会比在码头上马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）和桑达尔·皮查伊（Sundar Pichai）的景象更好。新年快乐！
  谁知道？诺亚·史密斯（Noah Smith）的彭博专栏文章《我们想要的并不总是能让我们开心》。
 大西洋网站上的弗兰克·帕特诺伊（Frank Partnoy）提出的关于投资者如何利用实时卫星图像预测零售商的销售额（来自太空的股票精选）的有趣文章。
 凯莉·库罗根（Carrie Courogen）在“亮墙/暗室”网站上的美国梦，在诺拉·埃弗隆（Nora Ephron）的纽约进行。
note_en:
The tech giants’ law-free bonanza is coming to an end on both sides of the Atlantic, but let’s speed up the process
   I t’s always risky making predictions about the tech industry, but this year looks like being different, at least in the sense that there are two safe bets. One is that the attempts to regulate the tech giants that began last year will intensify; the second that we will be increasingly deluged by sanctimonious cant from  Facebook & co as they seek to avoid democratic curbing of their unaccountable power.
 On the regulation front, last year in the US,  Alphabet, Google’s corporate owner, found itself facing major antitrust suits from  38 states as well as from the Department of Justice. On this side of the pond, there are preparations for a  Digital Markets Unit with statutory powers that will be able to neatly sidestep the tricky definitional questions of what constitutes a monopoly in a digital age. Instead, the unit will decide on a case-by-case basis whether a particular tech company has “strategic market status” if it possesses “substantial, entrenched market power in at least one digital activity” or if it acts as an online “gateway” for other businesses. And if a company is judged to have this status, then penalties and regulations will be imposed on it.
  Over in Brussels, the  European Union has come up with a new two-pronged legal framework for curbing digital power – the  Digital Markets Act and the  Digital Services Act. The Digital Markets Act is aimed at curbing anti-competitive practices in the tech industry (like buying up potential competitors before they can scale up) and will include fines of 10% of global revenues for infringers. The Digital Services Act, for its part, will oblige social media platforms to take more responsibility for illegal content on their platforms – scams, terrorist content, images of abuse, etc – for which they could face fines of up to 6% of global revenue if they fail to police content adequately. So the US and UK approach focuses on corporate behaviour; the EU approach focuses on defining what is allowed legally.
 All of this action has been a long time coming and while it’s difficult to say exactly how it will play out, the bottom line is that the tech industry is – finally – going to become a regulated one. Its law-free bonanza is going to come to an end.
 Joe Biden’s choices for top staff in his administration include a depressing proportion of former tech company stalwarts
 The big question, though, is: when? Antitrust actions proceed at a glacial pace because of the complexity of the issues and the bottomless legal budgets of the companies involved. The judge in one of the big American antitrust cases against Google has said that he expects the case to get to court  only in late 2023 and then it could run for several years (as the Microsoft case did  in the 1990s).
 The problem with that, as the veteran anti-monopoly campaigner  Matt Stoller has pointed out, is that the longer monopolistic behaviour goes on, the more damage (eg, to advertisers whose revenue is being stolen and other businesses whose property is being appropriated) is being done. Google had $170bn in revenue last year and is growing on average at 10-20% a year. On a conservative estimate of 10% growth, the company will add another $100bn to its revenue by 2025, when the case will still be in the court. Facebook, says Stoller, “is at $80bn of revenue this year, but it is growing faster, so the net increase of revenue is a roughly similar amount. In other words, if the claims of the government are credible, then the lengthy case, while perhaps necessary, is also enabling these monopolists to steal an additional $100bn apiece.”
 What could speed up bringing these monopolists to account? A key factor is the vigour with which the US Department of Justice prosecutes its case(s). In the run-up to the 2020 election, the Democrats in Congress displayed an encouraging enthusiasm for  tackling tech monopolies, but Joe Biden’s choices for top staff in his administration include a depressing proportion of former tech company stalwarts. And his vice-president-elect,  Kamala Harris, consistently turned a blind eye to the anti-competitive acquisitions of the  Silicon Valley giants throughout her time as California’s attorney general. So if people are hoping for antitrust zeal from the new US government, they may be in for disappointment.
 Interestingly, Stoller suggests that another approach (inspired by the way trust-busters in the US acted in the 1930s) could have useful leverage on corporate behaviour from now on. Monopolisation isn’t just illegal, he points out, “it is in fact a crime, an appropriation of the rights and property of others by a dominant actor. The lengthy trial is essentially akin to saying that bank robbers getting to keep robbing banks until they are convicted and can probably keep the additional loot.”
 Since a basic principle of the rule of law is that crime shouldn’t pay, an addition of the possibility of criminal charges to the antitrust actions might, like the prospect of being hanged in the morning ( pace Dr Johnson), concentrate minds in Facebook, Google, Amazon and Apple. As an eternal optimist, I cannot think of a nicer prospect for 2021 than the sight of  Mark Zuckerberg and  Sundar Pichai in the dock – with Nick Clegg in attendance, taking notes. Happy new year!
  Who knew?  What We Want Doesn’t Always Make Us Happy is a great Bloomberg column by Noah Smith.
 Far out Intriguing piece on how investors are using real-time satellite images to predict retailers’ sales ( Stock Picks From Space), by Frank Partnoy on the  Atlantic website.
 An American dream  Lovely meditation on  Nora Ephron’s New York, by Carrie Courogen on the Bright Wall/Dark Room website.
title: 我想要的2021年就是让马克·扎克伯格出庭
title_en: All I want for 2021 is to see Mark Zuckerberg up in court

note_en:
Writing production-worthy Dockerfiles is, unfortunately, not as simple as you would imagine. Most Docker images in the wild fail here, and even professionals often [1] get [2] this [2] wrong [3].
 This repository has best-practices for writing Dockerfiles that I (@slimsag) have quite painfully learned over the years both from my personal projects and from my work @sourcegraph. This is all guidance, not a mandate - there may sometimes be reasons to not do what is described here, but if you  don't know then this is probably what you should be doing.
  Copy  the Dockerfile into your own project and follow the comments to create  your Dockerfile.
     Running containers as a non-root user substantially decreases the risk that container -> host privilege escalation could occur. This is an added security benefit. ( Docker docs,  Bitnami blog post)
  UIDs below 10,000 are a security risk on several systems, because if someone does manage to escalate privileges outside the Docker container their Docker container UID may overlap with a more privileged system user's UID granting them additional permissions. For best security, always run your processes as a UID above 10,000.
  Eventually someone dealing with your container will need to manipulate file permissions for files owned by your container. If your container does not have a static UID/GID, then one must extract this information from the running container before they can assign correct file permissions on the host machine. It is best that you use a single static UID/GID for all of your containers that never changes. We suggest  10000:10001 such that  chown 10000:10001 files/ always works for containers following these best practices.
  We suggest pinning image tags using a specific image  version using  major.minor, not  major.minor.patch so as to ensure you are always:
 Keeping your builds working ( latest means your build can arbitrarily break in the future, whereas  major.minor  should mean this doesn't happen)
  SHA pinning gives you completely reliable and reproducable builds, but it also likely means you won't have any obvious way to pull in important security fixes from the base images you use. If you use  major.minor tags, you get security fixes by accident when you build new versions of your image - at the cost of builds being less reproducable.
 Consider using  docker-lock: this tool keeps track of exactly which Docker image SHA you are using for builds, while having the actual image you use still be a  major.minor version. This allows you to reproduce your builds as if you'd used SHA pinning, while getting important security updates when they are released as if you'd used  major.minor versions.
 If you're a large company/organization willing to spin up infrastructure like image security scanners, automated dependency updating, etc. then  consider this approach as well.
  We suggest using  tini as the ENTRYPOINT in your Dockerfile, even if you think your application handles signals correctly. This can alter the stability of the host system and other containers running on it, if you get it wrong in your application. See the  tini docs for details and benefits:
 It protects you from software that accidentally creates zombie processes, which can (over time!) starve your entire system for PIDs (and make it unusable).
 It ensures that the default signal handlers work for the software you run in your Docker image. For example, with Tini, SIGTERM properly terminates your process even if you didn't explicitly install a signal handler for it.
 It does so completely transparently! Docker images that work without Tini will work with Tini without any changes.
      It allows people to ergonomically pass arguments to your binary without having to guess its name, e.g. they can write:
  If  CMD includes the binary name, then they must guess what your binary name is in order to pass arguments etc.
  If you want your Dockerfile to run on old/legacy Linux systems and Docker for Mac versions and wish to avoid DNS resolution issues, install bind-tools.
      Unfortunately, although Docker did add it natively,  it is optional (you have to pass  --init to the  docker run command). Additionally, because it is a feature of the runtime and e.g. Kubernetes will not use the Docker runtime but rather a different container runtime  it is not always the default so it is best if your image provides a valid entrypoint like  tini instead.
  It depends. We advise  major.minor pinning here because we believe it is the most likely thing that the average developer creating a new Docker image can effectively manage day-to-day that provides the most security. If you're a larger company/organization, you might consider instead however:
 Using one of the many tools for automated image vulnerability scanning, such as  GCR Vulnerability Scanning so you know  when your images have vulnerabilities.
  Using automated image tag update software,  such as Renovate to update your image tags and get notified.
 However, this obviously requires much more work and infrastructure so we don't advise it here with the expectation that  most people would pin a SHA and likely never update it again - thus never getting security fixes into their images.

特斯拉在2020年第四季度打破了以前的生产和交付记录，在过去三个月中向客户运送了18万多辆汽车。这比特斯拉第三季度的交付量高出30％，这本身是该公司的创纪录数字。
 特斯拉在2020年全年向客户交付了499,550辆汽车。这比特斯拉在2019年交付给客户的367,500辆汽车高出36％。
  特斯拉并没有完全实现该公司自己设定的今年交付50万辆汽车的目标。但是从字面上看，这是一个舍入误差，特斯拉2020年的表现仍然令人印象深刻。这场大流行使2020年对于汽车行业而言是异常艰难的一年。像美国大多数竞争对手一样，特斯拉的弗里蒙特工厂在3月下旬至5月中旬关闭。
 我们可以预期，特斯拉将在2021年交付更多的汽车。以每季度18万辆的第四季度速度，特斯拉到2021年应该能够从其弗里蒙特和上海工厂发货超过72万辆汽车。最重要的是，特斯拉的目标是2021年将在德克萨斯州和德国的新工厂上线。虽然特斯拉在2021年生产一百万辆汽车可能不可行，但到2022年应该很容易实现。埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）表示，他的目标是让特斯拉每年生产2000万辆汽车十年末的一年。
    特斯拉需要迅速扩张以证明其天价股票的合理性。特斯拉目前的股价使该公司的市值超过6000亿美元，远远超过其他任何汽车制造商。尽管事实上，丰田，大众汽车和通用汽车等行业领导者每年都生产数百万辆汽车，而特斯拉去年交付的汽车不到五十万辆。华尔街显然押注特斯拉将成长为一个巨大的市场，同时享有比现有公司更高的利润率。特斯拉将需要超过四个工厂才能满足这些高期望。
 尽管特斯拉的汽车出货量总体呈上升趋势，但该公司的高端Model S和Model X的销量在第四季度继续下滑。这些汽车多年来没有重大更新，因此消费者没有理由为它们购买较新的Model 3和Model Y溢价的产品。特斯拉在2020年第四季度交付了18,920辆这些高端汽车，低于2006年第四季度的19,450辆。 2019年第四季度，比2018年第四季度的27,550辆有所下降。
 这种趋势可能会在今年晚些时候随着Model S的预期更新而逆转。特斯拉也有几款新车正在筹备中。特斯拉的Semi卡车，Cyber​​truck皮卡和新型Roadster跑车都计划于2021年开始生产，尽管其中一到两个似乎不会在2022年之前真正亮相。这条生产线将使特斯拉能够继续提高产量，而不必担心市场饱和。
note_en:
Tesla  smashed previous production and delivery records in the fourth quarter of 2020, shipping more than 180,000 vehicles to customers over the last three months. That's 30 percent higher than Tesla's Q3 deliveries, which itself was a record figure for the company.
 For 2020 as a whole, Tesla delivered 499,550 vehicles to customers. This is 36 percent higher than the 367,500 vehicles Tesla delivered to customers in 2019.
  Tesla didn't  quite hit the company's self-imposed target of half a million deliveries for the year. But the miss is literally a rounding error and Tesla's 2020 performance is still pretty impressive. The pandemic made 2020 an unusually challenging year for the auto industry. Tesla's Fremont factory—like those of most competitors in the US—was  closed between late March and mid-May.
 We can expect Tesla to deliver many more vehicles in 2021. At its Q4 rate of 180,000 vehicles per quarter, Tesla should be able to ship more than 720,000 vehicles from its Fremont and Shanghai factories in 2021. On top of that, Tesla is aiming to bring new factories online in Texas and Germany in 2021. While it might not be feasible for Tesla to produce a million vehicles in 2021, it should be easily in reach for 2022. Elon Musk has  said he aims to have Tesla producing 20 million cars a year by the end of the decade.
    Tesla needs to expand rapidly to justify its astronomical stock price. Tesla's current stock price values the company at more than $600 billion dollars, far more than any other automaker. That despite the fact that industry leaders like Toyota, Volkswagen, and GM each produce millions of vehicles per year, while Tesla delivered fewer than half a million last year. Wall Street is apparently betting that Tesla will grow into a colossus while enjoying fatter profit margins than the incumbents. Tesla will need a lot more than four factories to meet those high expectations.
 While Tesla's vehicle shipments have been rising overall, sales of the company's high-end Model S and Model X continued to slump in Q4. These cars haven't had a significant refresh in years, giving consumers little reason to pay a premium for them over the newer Model 3 and Model Y. Tesla delivered 18,920 of these high-end vehicles in Q4 2020, down from 19,450 in Q4 2019 and  way down from 27,550 vehicles in Q4 2018.
 That trend may reverse later this year with an  expected refresh of the Model S. Tesla also has several new vehicles in the pipeline. Tesla's Semi truck, the Cybertruck pickup, and a new Roadster sports car are all scheduled to begin production in 2021—though it seems likely that one or two of them won't actually debut until 2022. The expanded product line should allow Tesla to continue boosting production without worrying about saturating the market.
title: 特斯拉在2020年交付了创纪录的500,000辆汽车
title_en: Tesla delivered a record 500,000 vehicles in 2020

据《纽约时报》报道，针对美国政府机构和私人公司的与俄罗斯有关的SolarWinds黑客事件可能比官员们最初意识到的还要糟糕，目前大约有250个联邦机构和企业受到影响。
 微软表示，这些黑客破坏了SolarWinds的Orion监视和管理软件，使他们能够“假冒该组织的任何现有用户和帐户，包括特权较高的帐户”。 《泰晤士报》报道，俄罗斯利用供应链的各个层次来访问这些机构的系统。
 《泰晤士报》报道，在这种情况下，预警指挥部发现网络指挥官和国家安全局放置在外部网络中以检测潜在的攻击，似乎已失败。此外，据《泰晤士报》报道，美国政府对保护11月大选免受外国黑客攻击的关注似乎已经夺走了资源，并将重点从软件供应链上转移了出去。而且，从美国内部进行攻击显然可以使黑客逃避国土安全部的侦查。
 微软本周早些时候表示，已经发现其系统“不仅存在恶意的SolarWinds代码，而且已经被渗透”。黑客能够“查看许多源代码存储库中的源代码”，但是授予访问权限的被黑帐户没有修改任何代码或系统的权限。但是，在一个好消息中，微软表示，它发现“没有证据表明可以访问生产服务或客户数据”，并且“没有迹象表明我们的系统曾被用来攻击其他系统。”
 参议院情报委员会高级成员参议员马克·沃纳（弗吉尼亚州参议员）告诉《纽约时报》，这次黑客袭击看上去“比他最初担心的要糟糕得多”。他说：“它的规模在不断扩大。” “很明显，美国政府没有这样做。”
note_en:
The Russia-linked SolarWinds hack which targeted US government agencies and private corporations may be even worse than officials first realized, with some 250 federal agencies and business now believed affected, the   New York Times reported.
 Microsoft has said the hackers compromised  SolarWinds’ Orion monitoring and management software, allowing them to “impersonate any of the organization’s existing users and accounts, including highly privileged accounts.” The  Times reports that Russia exploited layers of the supply chain to access the agencies’ systems.
 The  Times reports that early warning sensors that Cyber Command and the NSA placed inside foreign networks to detect potential attacks appear to have failed in this instance. In addition, it seems likely that the US government’s attention on protecting the November elections from foreign hackers may have taken resources and focus away from the software supply chain, according to the  Times. And conducting the attack from within the US apparently allowed the hackers to evade detection by the Department of Homeland Security.
 Microsoft  said earlier this week it had discovered its systems were infiltrated “beyond just the presence of malicious SolarWinds code.” The hackers were able to “view source code in a number of source code repositories,” but the hacked account granting the access didn’t have permission to modify any code or systems. However, in a small bit of good news, Microsoft said it found “no evidence of access to production services or customer data,” and “no indications that our systems were used to attack others.”
 Sen. Mark Warner (D-Virginia), ranking member on the Senate Intelligence Committee, told the  Times the hack looked “much, much worse” than he first feared. “The size of it keeps expanding,” he said. “It’s clear the United States government missed it.”
title: SolarWinds hack可能比最初担心的要糟得多
title_en: SolarWinds hack may be much worse than originally feared

note:
跳转至导航跳转至搜索社会崩溃（也称为文明崩溃）是人类复杂社会的衰落，其特征是丧失了文化认同感和社会经济复杂性，政府垮台和暴力上升。 [1]社会崩溃的可能原因包括自然灾害，战争，瘟疫，饥荒和人口减少。崩溃的社会可能会恢复到更原始的状态，被更强大的社会所吸收，或者完全消失。
 几乎所有文明都遭受了这种命运，无论其规模或复杂程度如何。但是，有些复兴并转变了，例如中国和埃及，而另一些则从未恢复，例如玛雅帝国和复活节岛上的文明。社会崩溃通常是一个快速过程，[1]但很少突然发生。 [2]然而，有些并没有崩溃，而只是逐渐消失，例如自1918年以来的大英帝国。[3]
 人类学家，（定量的）历史学家和社会学家对文明的崩溃提出了各种各样的解释，其中包括环境变化，资源枯竭，不可持续的复杂性，社会凝聚力下降，不平等加剧，认知能力长期下降，丧失等成因。创造力和不幸。 [1] [4] [5]但是，文化完全灭绝的情况很少；在大多数情况下，尽管老龄化的程度大大降低，但旧有灰烬产生的新社会显然是其后代。 [4]而且，一个崩溃的社会的影响，例如罗马帝国的影响，可能会在其死后很长一段时间内持续存在。 [6]
 历史，人类学，社会学和政治学专家研究了社会崩溃。最近，他们又加入了动力学和复杂系统研究领域的专家。 [7] [4]
   社会科学家卢克·肯普（Luke Kemp）从公元前3000年开始分析了数十种文明，将其定义为“具有农业，多个城市，在其地理区域具有军事优势并具有连续政治结构的社会”。到公元600年，并计算出一个文明的平均寿命接近340年。 [1]其中，最持久的是东北非洲的库什特王国（1,150年），非洲的阿克苏姆帝国（1,100年），南亚的吠陀文明和中美洲的奥尔梅克人（均为1,000年），而寿命最短的是元元朝（30），印度的南达帝国（24）和中国的秦朝（14）。 [8]
 复杂系统专家塞缪尔·阿伯斯曼（Samuel Arbesman）对帝国进行的统计分析表明，崩溃通常是随机事件，与年龄无关。这类似于进化生物学家所谓的“红皇后假说”，该假说断言，对于生态环境恶劣的物种而言，灭绝是一种持续的可能性。 [1]
  由于人类社会是复杂的系统，因此可能导致其衰落的共同因素-经济，环境，人口，社会和文化-可以级联成一个等级，以至于压倒一切原本可以维持稳定的机制。意外和突然的变化（专家称为非线性）是一些危险信号。 [3]在某些情况下，自然灾害（例如海啸，地震，流行病，大火或气候变化）可能会导致崩溃。马尔萨斯灾难，人口过多或资源枯竭等其他因素也可能是崩溃的直接原因。严重的不平等和公开的腐败可能与对已建立的政治机构的忠诚度缺乏相结合，导致被压迫的下层阶级崛起，并在革命中从较小的富裕精英手中夺取政权。社会发展形式的多样性对应于其失败的多样性。贾里德·戴蒙德（Jared Diamond）指出，由于森林砍伐，土壤肥力丧失，贸易限制和/或流行性暴力事件增多，社会也崩溃了。 [9] 
任何社会都有繁荣昌盛的时期。但是，当文明的高度下降如此剧烈时，人们可以放心地谈论它的崩溃。 [10]
  三十年战争摧毁了欧洲大部分地区，是十七世纪大危机期间众多政治动荡之一，与小冰河时期有因果关系。
 考古学家发现了在5000到4000年前的非洲和亚洲千年大干旱的迹象。绿色撒哈拉沙漠的干燥不仅使它变成了沙漠，而且破坏了南亚和东南亚的季风季节，并在东亚造成洪灾，从而阻止了成功的收获和复杂文化的发展。它是同时发生的，并且可能导致了阿卡德帝国在美索不达米亚和印度河流域文明的衰败。 [11]气候的剧烈变化被称为4.2公里事件。 [12]
 高度发达的印度河谷文明起源于公元前3000年左右。在现在的巴基斯坦境内，并于公元前1700年左右崩溃。由于印度河文字尚待破译，其灭亡的原因仍然是个谜，尽管有一些证据表明自然灾害。 [13]在公元前1900年开始出现逐渐下降的迹象，两个世纪后，大多数城市被废弃了。考古证据表明，人际暴力和麻风病和肺结核等传染病有所增加。 [14] [15]历史学家和考古学家认为，严重而持久的干旱以及与埃及和美索不达米亚的贸易下降导致这种文化的崩溃。 [16]还发现了地震证据。在现在位于内陆的Makran海岸的两个可能的海港站点上，也发现了海平面变化。地震可能通过直接震荡破坏，海平面变化或供水变化而导致多个地点的衰退。 [17] [18] [19]
 火山喷发会突然影响气候。在一次大喷发中，二氧化硫（SO 2）被排放到平流层中，在那里可以停留数年并逐渐被氧化成硫酸盐气溶胶。硫酸盐气溶胶具有高反射性，可减少入射的阳光并冷却地球表面。通过钻进冰川和冰盖，科学家可以访问大气成分历史档案。由内华达州里诺的沙漠研究所的约瑟夫·麦康奈尔领导的一个多学科研究小组推断，火山喷发发生在公元前43年，即公元前3月（3月15日）的尤利乌斯·凯撒遇难后一年，公元前44年。留下了权力真空，导致了流血的内战。根据历史记载，这也是一个天气恶劣，作物歉收，大饥荒和疾病的时期。来自全球不同地区的树木年轮和洞穴石笋的分析提供了补充数据。北半球更干燥，而南半球更湿。确实，希腊历史学家阿皮安（Appian）记录说，埃及没有洪水泛滥，埃及也面临着饥荒和瘟疫。罗马对埃及作为食物来源的兴趣增强了，而上述问题和内乱则削弱了埃及的抵抗能力。在公元前30年克娄巴特拉自杀后，它由罗马统治。虽然很难确定如果鄂木克火山（在现代阿拉斯加）没有爆发，埃及是否会成为罗马省会发生，但喷发可能会加速这一过程。 [20]
  更广泛地说，最近的研究指出，气候变化是中国，中东，欧洲和美洲历史社会衰落的关键因素。实际上，古气候温度重建表明，社会动荡，社会崩溃，人口崩溃和重大气候变化的历史时期经常同时发生。来自中国大陆和香港的一组研究人员能够在工业化之前建立气候变化与大规模人类危机之间的因果关系。短期危机可能是由于社会问题造成的，但气候变化是造成重大危机的最终原因，始于经济萧条。 [22]此外，由于农业高度依赖气候，最佳状态对区域气候的任何变化都可能导致农作物歉收。 [23]
 蒙古人的征服与13至14世纪北半球的降温时期相对应，当时中世纪的暖化时期已让位给小冰河时代，从而造成了生态压力。在欧洲，虽然凉爽的气候并没有直接助长黑死病，但它确实引起了战争，大规模移民和饥荒，使疾病更容易传播。 [23]

在十四世纪初，英国异常降雨，洪水泛滥，并屡屡遭受农作物歉收。许多牲畜饿死或淹死。粮食价格飞涨。当爱德华二世国王试图通过实行价格控制来纠正这种情况时，卖方只是拒绝以如此低的价格出售。无论如何，该法案在1316年被林肯议会废除了。不久，从平民到贵族的人们都发现自己缺乏食物。许多人乞讨，犯罪和吃动物，否则他们就不会吃。英格兰北部的人们不得不面对来自苏格兰的袭击。甚至有食人的报道。在欧洲大陆，情况至少同样糟糕。这场1315年至1317年的大饥荒与中世纪温暖期的结束和小冰河时代的开始相吻合。一些历史学家怀疑这种气候变化是由于1314年新西兰的塔拉威拉山喷发造成的。[41]然而，《大饥荒》只是该世纪袭击欧洲百年的灾难之一。战争和黑死病很快随之而来。 [41] [42]（另请参阅中世纪晚期的危机。）最近对树木年轮的分析补充了历史记录：1314–16年的夏天是700年以来有记录以来最湿的一些季节。 [42]
   从历史上看，农业的兴起导致了传染病的上升。 [43]与他们的狩猎聚会伙伴相比，农业社会往往是久坐的，人口密度较高，与牲畜经常接触，并且更容易受到污染的水源和较高浓度的垃圾的影响。卫生条件差，缺乏医学知识，迷信，有时甚至是多种灾害加重了这个问题。 [1] [43] [44]记者迈克尔·罗森瓦尔德（Michael Rosenwald）写道，＆＃34; ...历史表明，过去的流行病以深刻的方式重塑了社会。数以亿计的人死亡。帝国沦陷了。政府已经破裂。几代人被歼灭。＆＃34; [45]
 根据希腊医生Galen对症状的描述，包括咳嗽，发烧，（腹泻）腹泻，喉咙肿胀，口渴，现代专家发现，Antonine鼠疫（公元165-180年）的罪魁祸首是天花或麻疹。 [45] [46]这种疾病很可能始于中国，并通过丝绸之路传播到西方。罗马军队首先在东部感染了这种疾病，然后才返回家中。安东尼鼠瘟疫袭击了原始人口，死亡率令人恐惧。六分之一至七千万的人口死亡。罗马城市人满为患，卫生条件差，饮食不健康。他们很快成为震中。很快，这种疾病蔓延至高卢，并沿着罗马
